questions.question: What is the Milky Way?
options: One of many billions of galaxies in the Universe
The name of the Sun's planetary system
The only galaxy that contains stars
A red supergiant star at the centre of the Universe
questions.explanation:
According to the sources, the Milky Way is one of many billions of galaxies in the Universe. It contains our Solar System.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the Universe?
options: It consists of billions of galaxies
It consists of only one large galaxy
It is a collection of millions of solar systems with no galaxies
It is the name of our home galaxy
questions.explanation:
The sources state that the Universe consists of billions of galaxies, each containing billions of stars.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is meant by the term 'redshift' in astronomy?
options: Light from distant galaxies appears to have longer wavelengths
Light from stars turns red as they cool down to become white dwarfs
Light from approaching galaxies shifts to the red end of the spectrum
The process where a star expands to become a red giant
questions.explanation:
Redshift is defined as the observation that light emitted from stars in distant galaxies appears shifted to longer wavelengths (the red end of the spectrum) compared to light on Earth.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which theory is supported by the evidence of redshift?
options: The Big Bang Theory
The Theory of Constant Density
The Planetary Nebula Theory
The Nuclear Fission Theory
questions.explanation:
The sources note that redshift is evidence that the Universe is expanding and provides support for the Big Bang Theory.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is the standard unit used to measure interstellar and intergalactic distances?
options: Light-year
Kilometre
Megametre
Astronomical Unit
questions.explanation:
The light-year is used to measure the vast distances in space. One light-year is the distance light travels in one year.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
